title: ds-viz-rectangle - option matrix (dark)
description: Test bench for splunk.rectangle. Verifies every option from the 10.4 PDF: fillColor, fillOpacity, rx, ry, strokeColor, strokeDashStyle, strokeJoinStyle, strokeOpacity, strokeWidth. Demonstrates background cards, depth layers, pill shapes, dashed strokes, faint overlays, and KPI-card composition.

options.markdown:
# `splunk.rectangle` - option matrix

Rectangles are absolute-layout shapes with **9 options** total. They are the workhorse for **background cards**, **section dividers**, **depth layers**, **pill chips**, and **clickable hit-zones over images**.

All options from the **10.4 PDF**:
- `fillColor` (string, hex; theme default)
- `fillOpacity` (0 - 1)
- `rx` (corner radius, integer or %)
- `ry` (vertical corner radius, defaults to `rx`)
- `strokeColor` (string, hex; theme default)
- `strokeDashStyle` (number; pixel length of dashes & gaps; `0` = solid)
- `strokeJoinStyle` (`miter` | `bevel` | `round` | `arcs` | `miter-clip`)
- `strokeOpacity` (0 - 1)
- `strokeWidth` (1 - 25 px)

viz_default.title: 1. Default (theme defaults)
viz_default.description: No options set - uses theme.defaultFillColor / theme.defaultStrokeColor. Enterprise dark = #31373E fill, #C3CBD4 stroke.

viz_fill_custom.title: 2. Custom fillColor
viz_fill_custom.description: Solid card with a brand colour. Stroke transparent for a flat look. Most common usage.

viz_fill_opacity_half.title: 4. fillOpacity = 0.5
viz_fill_opacity_half.description: 50% fill - lets the background canvas bleed through. Useful for layered cards.

viz_fill_opacity_faint.title: 5. fillOpacity = 0.15 (faint)
viz_fill_opacity_faint.description: Near-transparent tint - section watermark or very subtle row stripe.

viz_rounded_subtle.title: 6. rx = 8 (subtle corners)
viz_rounded_subtle.description: Modern card - 8px radius is the most common choice for KPI cards and section backgrounds. ry inherits from rx by default.

viz_rounded_pill.title: 7. rx = 50 (pill / chip)
viz_rounded_pill.description: Rectangle becomes a pill / chip when rx >= height/2. Useful for status badges.

viz_stroke_basic.title: 8. strokeColor + strokeWidth
viz_stroke_basic.description: Outlined card. strokeWidth=2 + a brand stroke colour. Fill transparent.

viz_stroke_thick.title: 9. strokeWidth = 25 (max)
viz_stroke_thick.description: Maximum stroke width per the PDF. Anything above 25 is clipped. Use sparingly.

viz_stroke_dashed.title: 10. strokeDashStyle = 6
viz_stroke_dashed.description: Dashes & gaps both 6px. Often used for empty / placeholder slots in dashboards.

viz_stroke_join_round.title: 11. strokeJoinStyle = round
viz_stroke_join_round.description: Rounded join at the corners of the stroke path. Visible only at large strokeWidth + rx=0.

viz_stroke_opacity.title: 13. strokeOpacity = 0.4
viz_stroke_opacity.description: Strokes can be faded independently of fill. 40% stroke opacity gives a softer outlined card.

viz_card_background.title: 14. KPI card pattern
viz_card_background.description: Background card with a contrasting accent stroke and a singleValue layered on top. Demonstrates the canonical 'card + KPI' recipe.

viz_card_kpi.title: Throughput
viz_card_kpi.description: singleValue layered on top of viz_card_background.

options.markdown:
## Data-bound colour (DOS / `rangeValue`)

DOS = **Dynamic Options Syntax** — Splunk's pipe-language for binding any option string to a dataSource. The PDF says of `fillColor` and `strokeColor`: *"You may use a dataSource to apply the color."* This is the **status-card** pattern: a rectangle whose colour flips based on a metric (health, latency, error count) without any markdown / singlevalue overlay.

**Canonical editor form** (panels 15-19):

```
options.fillColor    = "> fillDataValue | rangeValue(fillColorEditorConfig)"
context.fillDataValue        = "> primary | seriesByType(\"number\") | lastPoint()"
context.fillColorEditorConfig = [ { from, to, value }, ... ]
```

Key rules: (1) the picker (`seriesByName` / `seriesByType`) returns a *series*, but `rangeValue` needs a *single number* — always reduce with `lastPoint()` (or `max()`, `min()`). (2) Splunk Studio's editor always writes the alias-in-`context` form; direct pipelines in `fillColor` won't roundtrip through the editor cleanly. (3) Threshold buckets in `context` are evaluated top-down — first match wins.

Thresholds across panels 15-17: red (< 70), amber (70-90), green (>= 90). All three feed the same config, only the dataSource differs (low=20 / mid=60 / high=95).

viz_dos_fill_low.title: 15. DOS fillColor - health = 20 (red)
viz_dos_fill_low.description: fillColor bound to the search via DOS. Value 20 hits the < 70 bucket -> red. Editor form: alias 'fillDataValue' in context with seriesByType + lastPoint(), then rangeValue(fillColorEditorConfig).

viz_dos_fill_mid.title: 16. DOS fillColor - health = 60 (still red, edge case)
viz_dos_fill_mid.description: Same DOS pattern, value 60. The first bucket {to: 70} matches everything below 70, so 60 also renders red. Demonstrates that overlapping rangeValue buckets are evaluated top-down.

viz_dos_fill_high.title: 17. DOS fillColor - health = 95 (green)
viz_dos_fill_high.description: Same DOS pattern, value 95. Hits the >= 90 bucket -> green. Note the seriesByName('health') alternative picker in the alias - identical effect to seriesByType because there is only one numeric column.

viz_dos_stroke.title: 18. DOS strokeColor - bound to health (95 -> green outline)
viz_dos_stroke.description: Same pattern but applied to the stroke alone. Useful for outlined status badges where the fill is your brand neutral. Note: the alias is named 'strokeDataValue' here so fill/stroke aliases never collide when both are bound.

viz_dos_both.title: 19. DOS fill + stroke - both bound (mid = red fill, light stroke)
viz_dos_both.description: Fill and stroke both DOS-bound to the same dataSource. Each option has its own alias and its own threshold table so they can flip independently. Value 60 hits the < 70 bucket on fill (red) and the >= 50 bucket on stroke (light grey). This is the canonical 'card with outlined emphasis' pattern.

viz_dos_token.title: 20. Token-driven fillColor - input flips colour
viz_dos_token.description: fillColor reads from a dashboard token via $colour_token$ interpolation. The dropdown above lets the viewer flip the card between red / amber / green without touching any data source. Useful for design previews and brand colour pickers.
